California
Fair Political
Practices Cornmission
March 21, 199 o
John Tenant, President
Pasadena Fire Fighters
Association Local No. 809
P.O. Box 5102
Pasadena, cA 91107
Re:
Your Request for Infornal Assistance
Our File No. I-9 0-111
Dear Ur. Tenant:
You are seeking advice regarding the respons ibilities 
and
duties of your organization's political action committee under the
campaign contributions and disclosure provisions of the Political
Ref-oru Act (the ttActtr ) . 1 Because your request f or advj.ce does not
refer to a specific governmental decision, lre are treating your
question as a request for informal assistance only, pursuant to
iegulation 18329 (copy enclosed) .2
The following advice is based upon the facts provided in your
letter of January 30, 1990, i-n your prior tetephone conversation
with the commission's Technical Assistance and Analysis Divj.sion,
and in your telephone conversation with this office on Febru-
ary 28, L99O.
1 Gorr".rr."nt code Sections 81ooo-91015. A11 statutory references
are to the covernment code unless otherwise indicated. 
Commission
regrulations appear at 2 california Code of Regulations Section
18000, gE seo. A11 references to regnrlations are to Title 2, Di-
vision 5 of the California code of Regrulations.
2 Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
innunity provided by an opinion or fornal written advice. (covern-
ment code section 83114,' 2 cal. Code of Regs. Section
1832e (c) (3) . )
428 J street, suite 8oo . P.o. Box 8o? o sacramento cA 958o4-o8oz . (916) 3 zz-s66o


File No. r-90-111
Page 2
OUESTION
Does the Act permit the Pasadena Firefighters Association
broad based political action comrnittee (the rtfirefighters associ-
ation PACI,) to sponsor school conpetitions on various civ5.c top-
ics, and provide the winners of the coupetitions with air trans-
portation to and accomnodations in sacramento to enable theE to
neet rrith their legislators?
CONCLUSION
The firefighters association PAc nay undertake such an activ-
itv.
FACTS
The Pasadena Firefighters Association has established a broad
based political action courmittee that has accunulated and
continues to collect funds. The firefighters association PAC is
interested in participating in various charitable and public rela-
tions activities in addition to those directly related to politi-
caI campaigns.
One such contenplated public relations effort is for the
firefighters association PAc to sponsor courpetitions in the
schools as a Deans of heightening community awareness on various
civic issues (finishing school, crirne, drugs, etc.). 
The fire-
fighters association PAc, through poster and essay contests, Irou1d
have local officials select student winners, to whom recognition
rdould be gi-ven by providing then with air transportation to and
accommodati.ons in Sacramento to enable then to rneet with their
elected legislators.
It is understood that the firefighters association PAc is
interested in participating in this type of charitable and public
relations activity as a means by which it can increase its vis-
ibility 
in the conmunity and enhance its reputation with state and
local elected officials.
ANALYSTS
The Act pernits certain uses of canpaign funds held by candi-
dates, elected officials, 
and political action and other coumit-
tees (Section 85800(a)) i the specified uses serve as a gruide to
practices not otherwise expressly nenti,oned. (Section 85802.)
fhe Act defines the tertr r.giftir to mean a paynent to the ex-
tent that consideration of equal or greater value is not received.
( section 82028 .')
The winners of the school conpetitions, vho are provided
transportation to and accommodations in Sacramento, would be


File No. I-9 0-111
Page 3
deened to have received gifts frorn the firefighters 
PAc for pur-
poses of the Act. (In re Gutierrez (L977, 3 FPPC ops. 44, copy
encLosed. )
The expenditure of campaign funds for rrpersonal giftstt is
pernitted only rrhen the gift is directly related to a political,
legislative, or governmental purpose. (Section 85802(f) (1).)
Your inquiry, therefore, requires a determination whether the
firefighters' 
PAc sponsorship of the conpetitions, and arrarding
the student winners travel to and acconmodations in Sacranento to
enable then to ureet with their legislative representatives, is di-
rectly related to a political, 
legTislative, or governmental pur-
Pose.
we conclude that the gifts provided to the student winners
are pernitted under section 85802(f) (L) because they are directly
related to a political, 
legislative, or governmental Purpose. The
sponsorship of the conpetitions is in part undertaken for the
purposes of assisting, and in fact rri11 assist, the firefighters'
PAC in realizing various political 
and public relations objec-
tives, including an increase in the organization's visibility, 
an
enhanced reputation in the community, and an anticipated rise in
the Ieve1 of contributions to the organization itself. 
For these
reasons it appears that the gifts to the student winners would
neet the requirenents of Section 85802(f) (1).
Because it has only recently assumed jurisdiction over the
laws regarding the pernissible uses of carnpaign funds, the conmis-
sion will begin consideration of regulations interpreting these
Iaws in the near future. At that tirne, it is possible that the
Conmission nay choose to adopt regTulations vhich rnay require rnodi-
fication of the advice given in this letter.
I trust this letter has provided you rrith the gruidance you
requested. If you have any further questions regarding this mat-
ter please contact ne at (916) 322-5091.
S incerely,
Kathryn E. Donovan
General
l
Divis ion

Enc■osures
bnathan SI Ro
LegaL
